channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: New York prison guards fired for ignoring deal to end strike, thousands set to lose health insurance
item.description: New York officials have started to fire state prison guards who failed to comply with a deal to end their illegal labor strike, which is now in its third week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pauses US military aid to Ukraine while pressuring Zelenskyy to move toward quick end to war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday directed a “pause” to U.S. assistance to Ukraine as he seeks to pressure Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to engage in negotiations to end the war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, ZEKE MILLER and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trans vegan cult member linked to Vermont border agent killing was person of interest in parents' murders
item.description: A double murder of a Pennsylvania couple in 2022 has been linked to the recent fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah mommy blogger Ruby Franke's power, public image allowed child abuse to go 'unchecked': expert
item.description: Disgraced parenting blogger and mom of six Ruby Franke's "power" and public image" allowed her crimes against her children to go "unchecked," according to a defense attorney.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US tariffs on Canada and Mexico take effect, as China takes aim at US farm exports
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s long-threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico went into effect Tuesday, putting global markets on edge and setting up costly retaliations by the United States’ North American allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, PAUL WISEMAN and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Animal skins, bells, ritual chaos: Ancient burnout remedy is still at the heart of Greece’s carnival
item.description: DISTOMO, Greece (AP) — Feeling overwhelmed by everyday obligations or doom-scrolling? The ancient Greeks had a remedy for burnout still practiced annually by their rural descendants.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS and PETROS GIANNAKOURIS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A first-term Michigan senator is set to deliver the Democratic response to Trump's speech
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — The Democratic response to President Donald Trump’s first joint congressional address of his second presidency will be delivered by a freshman Michigan senator who built an extensive national security background before entering politics less than a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Census Bureau under Trump seeks permission to delete questions about gender identity
item.description: The U.S. Census Bureau under the Trump administration has sought permission to delete questions about gender identity from a monthly survey.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Orleans to celebrate Mardi Gras Day with costumes and revelry despite weather concerns
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Thousands of revelers adorned in beads and outlandish costumes will fill the streets of New Orleans as the city celebrates Mardi Gras Day despite anticipated severe storms.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How South Carolina's execution of a condemned killer by firing squad will unfold
item.description: Three volunteer South Carolina prison employees will be the firing squad used to execute condemned killer Brad Sigmon on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis suffers new breathing crises, is back on noninvasive ventilation, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis suffered two new acute respiratory crises Monday and was put back on noninvasive mechanical ventilation, in another setback to his battle to fight pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Trump pauses aid to Ukraine amid clash with Zelensky
item.description: President Trump has ordered a pause on U.S. aid going to Ukraine in its war against Russia, a consequential move as he clashes with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky about the path to ending the conflict. "The President has been clear that he is focused on peace.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: WAUKEGAN, Ill. (AP) — An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 leaders of a group suspected of smuggling 20,000 immigrants are arrested in Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Federal authorities in Los Angeles arrested two alleged leaders of a criminal organization suspected of smuggling 20,000 people from Guatemala to the U.S. and charging each person as much as $18,000 to get them into the country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Popular clothing store chain slashing hundreds of workers, closing California HQ
item.description: One of the most well-known clothing brands in the United States, Forever 21, is laying off nearly 700 workers in California and Pennsylvania and closing its headquarters in the Golden State.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: As 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada are set to take effect on Tuesday, Hispanic-owned businesses and companies that depend on cross-border trade are already passing higher prices onto consumers and preparing to sharply reduce imports.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: GOP rep introduces measure putting Trump face on $100 bill
item.description: First-term GOP Rep. Brandon Gill (Texas) has introduced a measure that would require all $100 bills to have President Trump’s face on them.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump says 25% tariffs on Mexican and Canadian imports will start Tuesday, with 'no room' for delay
item.description: President Donald Trump says 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada will start Tuesday, sparking renewed fears of a North American trade war.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Killer whales amaze Seattle onlookers with a rarely seen bird hunt
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A pod of orcas swam close to shore and amazed onlookers in Seattle by treating the whale watchers to the rare sight of the apex predators hunting a bird.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cat food recalled due to bird flu risk
item.description: The FDA is alerting pet owners that some raw cat food is being recalled because it could be contaminated with bird flu.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, NewsNation

item.title: In Memoriam snubs: Late actors who weren't honored at the Oscars
item.description: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, Tony Todd, and Quincy Jones were some of the notable figures not included in the In Memoriam segment at the 2024 Academy Awards, sparking outrage among their fans.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Waltz likens Zelenskyy to an 'ex-girlfriend' after Oval Office spat
item.description: National security adviser Mike Waltz compared Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to an “ex-girlfriend.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon boy, 2, goes missing from family front yard, sparking massive search
item.description: Dane Paulsen, a 2-year-old boy, disappeared in broad daylight while playing in his family's front yard in Oregon on Saturday, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NOAA firings spark fears about long-term damage
item.description: The Trump administration has made drastic cuts to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) that threaten to impact weather forecasting and other key services provided by the agency.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: 175 wildfires in North and South Carolina force some evacuations
item.description: Wildfires in North and South Carolina have scorched thousands of acres as crews battling more than 175 blazes face dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's next first speech to Congress is bound to have little resemblance to his last first one
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation will hear a new president sing a far different tune in his prime-time address before Congress on Tuesday night. Some Americans will lustily sing along. Others will plug their ears.
item.dc:creator: CALVIN WOODWARD, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Latest: The 97th Academy Awards are underway
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The 97th Academy Awards have kicked off at the Dolby Theatre in Los Angeles. “Emilia Pérez” leads the pack with 13 Oscar nominations, but “Wicked” and “The Brutalist” are close behind. The 2025 Oscars opened with its biggest musical voices.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee could add 'covenant marriage' with proposed bill
item.description: One Tennessee lawmaker would like to add a new kind of marriage to the books through a bill filed in the General Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Erin McCullough, Fox 5

item.title: Threatening letter sent to northern Colorado family over MAGA banner
item.description: A Severance family says they recently received a threatening letter in the mail, in response to a "Make America Great Again" banner posted in their front yard.
item.dc:creator: Greg Nieto, ABC 4
